title: Woodsiopsis plummerae
intro: Woodsiopsis plummerae, also known as Plummer's Fern, is a species of fern in the family Woodsiaceae. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine to alleviate various health issues, including respiratory problems, inflammation, and fever, due to its expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Woodsiopsis plummerae include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Plummer's Fern is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, as well as being used in poultices and salves to treat skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Woodsiopsis plummerae is classified as a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This family is a part of the larger group of plants known as the leptosporangiate ferns. The Dryopteridaceae family is further classified under the division Pteridophyta, which encompasses all types of ferns. Woodsiopsis plummerae is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history as a member of this family.
